The introduction is already out there. I have like 20 more to publish over the next few weeks, so stay tuned. For those who are looking to try this out on BTP ABAP trial, we will have to wait a little bit longer. We are currently looking for a viable solution to make that happen and I'll be sure to update you all on the progress. So stay tuned and bye for now.

[DJ] As you've no doubt heard this week, not least from my good friend and colleague Rich in this very same SAP developer news episode, Joule is now GA for ABAP. If you're an ABAP developer, this is a major milestone. but it's also a great opportunity to make sure your skills are up to date and your knowledge is fresh. Now, the ABAP CONF conference, a great friend and ally of the Code Connect week that takes place annually, has a YouTube channel with plenty of event session replays. Why not head over there and update your knowledge from experts in the field? Joule, of course, is great, but there are also some great gems over on this YouTube channel. See what I did that? I'll be learning.

[DJ] Beyond the SAP BTP cockpit, there are different ways to manage resources on the platform. There's the BTP CLI, of course, there's a family of APIs, and for open declarative management of resources, there's Terraform, and specifically the Terraform provider for SAP BTP. Now, the Terraform provider team have been working hard to create content for you to get started. And in fact, they've recently finished and published a course, getting started with Terraform on SAP BTP. It's around two to three hours, it's free and it's great content. Definitely recommended. Talking of Terraform on SAP BTP, the Terraform exporter for SAP BTP has just seen a new release published. Now, just to remind you, the Terraform exporter for SAP BTP is a way for you to get started with existing resources. We all have existing resources on SAP BTP, right? Anyway, for more details on this update and what it brings, check out Christian blog post.

[Shilpa] Hello, ABAP developers. You might know about CDS external entities, which allows ABAP applications to retrieve data from external system using SQL and make it available within the CDS infrastructure. Now, external system can be SAP HANA or non-SAP HANA data system or remote ASABAP. With the latest release of SAP BTP ABAP environment, 2502, these external entities can be defined as writable. Now, writable external entities can modify the data on the external systems using SQL operations like insert, update, modify, and delete. So in addition to the read-only scenarios, it is possible for the ABAP applications to modify the data on the external system using SQL. Now, writing is only possible via service connection in order to decouple the remote transaction from the local transaction. The syntax and more details is in the help document. I will leave the link in the description.

[Kevin] So one tool I regularly use is the SAP Business Technology Platform Solution Diagram repository. And for those who don't know what I'm talking about, the SAP BTP Solution Diagram repository is a collection of helpful icons, shapes, and other useful tools to easily create beautiful solution diagrams. And they regularly ship updates to that. And right now, we got a new update, allowing you to resize icons, which is great, and they also enhanced the SAP BTP foundational icons with updated SAP Cloud Identity Service icons and more. They added also document grounding to SAP BTP AI and extensibility service for BTP to SAP BTP AppDev Automation. And finally, they also added two new libraries. One is the SAP generic icons.

entry.title: “With ‘Joule for Developers’ We Have a Treasure in Our Hands”
entry.content:
Sonja Liénard, head of SAP BTP ABAP, has a long history in ABAP development and has held various roles at SAP. She has worked on the ABAP database interface, the ABAP Kernel, and ABAP Developer Tools.

Her teams are working all-out to ensure that the new capability in SAP Build released this week for general use by customers and partners – Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities (known internally as “Joule for ABAP”) – is continually tested and enhanced. In this Q&A, Sonja gives SAP News a look at the new capability and talks about its impact for both developers and SAP. 

SAP News: More than 7,000 ABAP developers at SAP have been testing the AI extensions for over three months now, along with 120 people from 13 pilot customers and partners. What does the support from Joule look like and what feedback have you received?

Sonja: The feedback has been very positive. Joule for developers can answer questions about programming, explain code, suggest code auto-completion, and perform unit tests. [See the box at the end of the article for explanations.]

Our more than 150 developers from a variety of departments who took part in our initial beta test rated satisfaction levels from 70% to more than 80% in all of these areas. The new AI features make their work much easier. As such, we are seeing very high recommendation rates.

The feedback from our customers and partners was even more positive: they are eager to start using the tools in their systems on an everyday basis. The many inquiries about additional use cases that we have received show us that our customers and partners are using Joule for developers intensively and have very high expectations of it.

Where isn’t the AI working satisfactorily yet? What needs to be improved?

We’re talking about an initial delivery – so it’s completely normal that we’re working on ongoing improvements. In particular, the predictive code completion was missing a key improvement during the test phase, but now has it. Our experts and data scientists worked all-out to achieve that. And the AI models are being trained on specific use cases so they can deliver the best results.

It’s also important to remember that Joule can’t read minds. The quality of the proposed code depends on how completely and accurately the developers specify their requirements. Without sufficient comments or specifications, the results will never be perfect – just like with other AI use cases.

We announced general availability of AI support for developers this week. Why is AI support for ABAP developers so important for SAP from a strategic perspective?

There are several reasons. First of all, because the market has been waiting for Joule for developers for quite a long time and there’s a lot of demand. It can significantly boost efficiency in development, along with user satisfaction.

Secondly, our model is unique because it’s based on millions of lines of ABAP code that only SAP has. So, we have something really valuable here! This model is truly one of a kind in this form.

Last but not least, Joule for developers will help SAP achieve its strategic goals, especially with regard to cloud strategy and increasing cloud revenue. A true ace up our sleeve, indeed.

What are the next steps? Is there anything else you’d like to say to our developers?

After general availability (GA), the next important step is the enhancement of the custom code migration tools with AI support. We’ve put together a dedicated team to concentrate on training the model for this migration, and it aims to truly transform from classic ABAP to ABAP Cloud.

This is a groundbreaking approach because it simplifies and accelerates custom code migration – that is, the transformation from classic ABAP, some of it developed or modified directly by our customers, into ABAP Cloud. This step supports RISE with SAP, for instance, as well as the general adoption of our cloud portfolio, and contributes directly to meeting SAP’s cloud revenue targets. It’s a real treasure!

And to the developers: if you’re not working with the tool yet, give it a try! We’re sure that it won’t just be a big help; it’ll be a lot of fun, too. Find out about innovations and info sessions. We’d be happy to get your feedback.
@lienards 
_____________________

This is how Joule for developers supports you: 

  • Chat lets developers interact directly with the tool, similar to Google and Copilot, to ask questions and get support for coding. 
  • Explain helps developers understand the existing code and development objects by giving them explanations of selected coding. 
  • Predictive code completion suggests lines of code for potential implementation, based on comments and the previously entered specifications. Developers can accept or reject this ghost text.
  • Unit test generation automates the generation of unit tests for ABAP classes and core data services and helps improve them, helping developers make sure that the code functions properly. 

    This interview was first published at SAP One, SAP's employee portal.

entry.title: How to build a responsive UI for a RAP business object that starts an asynchronous process
entry.content:
Introduction

Are you looking for a way to have responsive UI that starts an asynchronous process? 

With the background processing framework (BGPF) there is a way to start an asynchronous process from withing the RAP framework. This also allows to start processes that perform a commit work.

In this blog post I will show an easy way to start an asynchronous process using BGPF (the calculation of an inventory entry) and an easy way how to update the UI with the result of the calculation since with the latest release of the SAP BTP ABAP Environment a new feature was released which allows to refresh the UI of a SAP Fiori Elements applications by simply raising an event.   

 

Prerequisites

  • BGPF is available for private cloud and on premise SAP S/4HANA Systems as of release 2023 
  • Event Drive Side Effects are currently only available in SAP BTP ABAP Environment and SAP S/4HANA public cloud ABAP Environment. We plan to make this available for on premise and private cloud systems as of release 2025

Overview

A detailed introduction of the background processing can be found in the following blog post here Introducing the Background Processing Framework .

The background processing framework is the technology of choice when you want to start asynchronous processes in ABAP cloud rather than using application jobs which are meant to be used for long running and recurring processes. 

The generell problem when starting a long running process from within a stateless UI was that the UI could not be updated asynchronously unless you wanted to block the usage of the UI until the long running process has finished.

The source code of this sample class can be found in SAP-samples on Github.   

 Event driven side effects now allow to update the UI of a Fiori Elements application when an asynchronous process has finished. 

When working on the implementation a colleage of mine, @Martin_Müller , showed me an elegant way how a process can be started using BGPF by simply using a class that uses a class with a static method that takes the key of the updated entity of our RAP BO as a parameter in the save sequence of our RAP busines object.

The whole BGPF processing is done within this class and it thus makes the use of BGPF much easier and you end up with a simple call in an additional or unmanaged save in your behavior implementation class like the following:

 

 

 

DATA(bgpf_process_name) = zbgpfcl_calc_inventory_006=>run_via_bgpf( i_rap_bo_entity_key = update_inventory-%key ).

 

 

 

 

 

Implementation details

You can find the source code of a simple sample application in the following GitHub repository.

SAP-samples - How to use BGPF and events for side effects in ABAP RESTful programming model 

Further information

Event Drive Side Effects

  • SAP Online Help

Background Processing Framework

  • Blog Post: Introducing the Background Processing Framework 
  • SAP Online Help The RAP Transactional Model and the SAP LUW

entry.title: Be Part of Tomorrow: Join SAP's Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1, 2025!
entry.content:
We’re thrilled to announce the launch of the first cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2025, featuring 50 exciting new projects aimed at fostering innovation and collaboration with our valued SAP customers and partners.

In today’s fast-paced business world, staying ahead is more important than ever. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative presents a unique opportunity to gain early insights into SAP’s product developments. By participating, you’ll work directly with our developers and product managers, helping shape the future of our products.

Register until March 28, 2025! Don’t miss out on the opportunity to have your voice heard and influence the next generation of SAP solutions.

To help you explore the available projects, we’ve compiled a detailed list for your convenience. Check out the full list here or follow the links below to discover the exciting opportunities:

ABAP

  • ABAP Cloud Development Assistant
  • OData UI Service from scratch Generator

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • BTP AI based Data Anonymization Service
  • Create AI-driven extensions and automations with SAP Build
  • Improving the Admin's and Solution Architect's Experience on SAP BTP
  • SAP BPM, SAP BRM and SAP Process Orchestration migration to Cloud
  • SAP BTP Cockpit, Cloud Foundry UIs
  • SAP BTP Usability Event - User research project
  • SAP Business Technology Platform: Multi-Region Desaster Recovery
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Provisioning, Integration with Joule 
  • SAP Integration Suite

SAP Concur

  • SAP Concur - AI Managed Expense Reports
  • SAP Concur - Dynamic Card Management – Virtual Credit Cards
  • SAP Concur Expense - Web Services for Financial Integration
  • SAP Concur Mobile app and the sign-in user experience
  • SAP Concur Solutions – All About E-Receipts
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Customer Managed Keys for granular control over data
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Sponsored Guest Feature
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Web Notifications Center and Preferences

SAP Customer Experience

  • Research project into a potential loyalty cloud solution
  • SAP CX AI Toolkit

Data & Analytics

  • Redesigning the Dashboard Experience
  • SAP HANA Cloud Elastic Compute Node
  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Bank Relationship Management: Improving Overview, Services & Fee, and Interest Calculations
  • Fiori First for Sales Order Management
  • Identifying and resolving critical situations in a production planners day in S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Identity and Access Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Insights into Business processes with Business Event Logging
  • Joule and GenAI use cases in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
  • New Financial Reporting for Enterprise Project (Project Booklet) including ETO
  • Planned New Solution for Cost Component Breakdown
  • Project staffing, consider dependencies across different roles in a project
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Product Change Management
  • Simplification of transports in SAP S/4HANA® Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape
  • Usability Improvements for managing attachments in S/4HANA business applications

SAP Globalization

  • Australia and New Zealand: GST requirement for Down payments
  • Australia: Modified Payment Times Report - AASB 8 and Taxable Payments Annual Report
  • ISO 21378 Audit Data Collection Standard
  • Planned enhancements to Run statutory reports to handle Draft Report reconciliation
  • Sales and Use Tax Reporting in United States - User Research
  • Sales and Use Tax payments in United States

Lifecycle Management

  • DevOps with ABAP

SAP Integration Experience Feedback goes SAP Customer Engagement Initiative

  • Assisted Assessment of Aligned Business APIs for External Workforce
  • Assisted Assessment of Aligned Business APIs for Hire to Retire Cloud
  • Assisted Assessment of Aligned Business APIs for Source-to-Pay

Others

  • Research into AI-Assisted User Learning Rollout for Adoption
  • SAP Reference Architecture in SAP LeanIX
  • Shape the future of SAP Cloud ALM Configuration & Security Analysis
  • The AI-Driven Future of New Product Introduction (NPI)

We believe that collaboration is key to sparking innovation. By working alongside our customers and partners, we can jointly shape the future of SAP’s products and transform business operations.

Let’s take this journey together. Together, we have the power to drive progress and transform industries.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2025!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: SAP Developer News February 27th, 2025
entry.content:
PODCAST: 

https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/02/27/sap-developer-news-february-27th-2025/

DESCRIPTION 

New Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities videos!!! 

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/the-power-of-joule-for-developers-abap-ai-capabilities/ba-p/14019716?source=social-Developer_Community-Upskill-Video-Educate%2FEnable-Global-N%2FA-SAP.com&campaigncode=CRM-YA23-SMS-1941768&sprinklrid=16176097274 
  • https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQAt9lvPw0gF4E3nwbJD0EUe 

 SAP Integration Suite Onboarding Webinar 

  • Event link: https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-events/sap-integration-suite-onboarding-webinar-global/ev-p/14024129 
  • Registration Page: https://www.sap.com/events/2025-03-18-online-sap-integration-suite-onboarding-webinar.html 

 SAP Inside Track Bangalore - This Saturday 

  • Registration Page: https://konfhub.com/sap-inside-track-bengaluru-march-2025-business-ai-edition 
  • Event link: https://community.sap.com/t5/bengaluru-events/announcing-sap-inside-track-bengaluru-1st-march-2025/ec-p/13990380 

 How to build a responsive UI for a RAP business object that starts an asynchronous process 

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-build-a-responsive-ui-for-a-rap-business-object-that-starts-an/ba-p/14027487 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 New Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities videos!!! 

1:14 SAP Integration Suite Onboarding Webinar  

2:07 SAP Inside Track Bangalore - This Saturday 

3:04 How to build a responsive UI for a RAP business object that starts an asynchronous process 

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP developer News for February 27th, 2025.

[Rich] Hey friends, Rich Heilman here. Last week, I told you about the release of Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities, and I just wanted it to make you aware of some two-minute of ABAP cloud videos that we are publishing over the next few weeks. These videos are specifically around the Joule for developer ABAP AI capabilities and should give you a quick overview of what it is and how to use it and some tips and tricks as well. I've created a special playlist on the developer's YouTube channel for your convenience, and I'll post a link in the comments as well. The first few are out there now with a fun little introduction, and then we get right into the good stuff in the next episodes of the series, introducing you to Joule and how to get started using it today. These demos are coming straight out of our development organization by our developers for our developers. So check them out today. Bye for now.

[Ajay] Hello, everyone. If you are interested in SAP Integration Suite or curious about what it is, we have got a great new webinar coming up that I would like to tell you about. You can join the global webinar on SAP Integration Suite onboarding on March 18th, 2025 from 3 to 4PM CET. This session is geared towards all personas looking to get started with SAP Integration Suite to help drive your digital transformation. You will get an overview of all the available SAP Integration Suite capabilities and learn how to activate them. The session covers frequently asked questions, fundamentals and beginner tips to help you get started with SAP Integration Suite. We look forward to seeing you at this event. The registration link for this webinar is available in the description.

[Mamikee] This Saturday, March 1st, 2025, SAP Inside Track, Bengaluru is bringing together developers, experts, and innovators for a full day of learning, networking, and hands-on AI experiences live and in-person. This is your chance to explore cutting-edge AI solutions across SAP BTP, S/4 HANA, Ariba, concur, and Success Factor. You'll hear from directly from top SAP experts about the future of Enterprise AI and discover real-run success stories from businesses already leveraging AI to drive innovation. You'll get handled experience in interactive workshops, explore live demos, and see how AI seamlessly integrates into SAP solutions. This is your opportunity to connect with SAP professionals, developers, and AI inclusives all in one place. And remember, this is a in-person-only event, so don't miss out. You can secure your spot in the link below.

[Rich] Wait, wait, wait, wait. I'm still Rich Heilman, and I forgot to tell you about a new blog post from our good friend Andre Fischer. And in his blog post, Andre shows us how to build a responsive user interface for RAP business objects that start as an asynchronous process. So in this blog post, Andre shows us an easy way to start that process using the background process framework and shows us an easy way to update the user interface with the result thanks to a new feature which has recently been released which allows to refresh the user interface of an SAP Fiori Elements application by simply raising an event. So very cool stuff here. Make sure to check out Andre's blog post today and I'll link to it in the description and bye for now. And I'm really going to go this time. Bye-bye. Thank you.

entry.title: What is SAP RISE ? Technical Architect Perspective to ABAP, migrations-options, tools
entry.content:
RISE with SAP is a comprehensive, business-transformation-as-a-service offering from SAP designed to help organizations modernize their enterprise systems, migrate to the cloud, and adopt SAP S/4HANA as their core ERP platform. Launched in early 2021, it’s a bundled solution that combines software, services, and infrastructure into a single subscription-based contract, aimed at simplifying the journey to an intelligent enterprise. It’s particularly relevant for companies transitioning from legacy SAP systems like ECC to the cloud-native, HANA-powered S/4HANA, while leveraging advanced technologies like AI, analytics, and process automation.

Core Concept of RISE with SAP

RISE with SAP is not just a product but a holistic program that shifts the traditional on-premise ERP model to a cloud-centric, managed-service approach. It’s built around three key pillars:

 

  1. Move to SAP S/4HANA: Encourages adoption of S/4HANA as the digital core, replacing older systems like ECC, with a focus on cloud deployment (public, private, or hybrid).
  2. Cloud Transformation: Provides the infrastructure and tools to operate in a cloud environment, reducing the burden of managing hardware and software stacks.
  3. Intelligent Enterprise: Integrates advanced capabilities (e.g., AI, machine learning, IoT) to streamline processes and drive innovation.

The “RISE” acronym reflects its promise: Realize value, Innovate rapidly, Simplify operations, and Evolve continuously. (** this is not official acronym from SAP, it is derived from what its DNA) 

Key Components of RISE with SAP

RISE with SAP bundles several elements into one offering, managed under a single SLA (Service Level Agreement) with SAP or its partners. Here’s what’s included:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud: The ERP software itself, available in:

  1. Public Cloud: Fully managed by SAP, multi-tenant, with standardized processes.
  2. Private Cloud: Single-tenant, customizable, hosted on hyperscalers like AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud.
  3. Hybrid: Combines on-premise and cloud elements. Runs on SAP HANA, enabling real-time data processing and simplified data models.

RISE Tools and Services:

  1. SAP Business Process Intelligence (BPI): Analyzes and optimizes processes before migration (e.g., using tools like Signavio).
  2. SAP Readiness Check: Assesses legacy systems for S/4HANA compatibility, flagging custom code and simplification items.
  3. SAP Migration Cockpit: Automates data migration from ECC to S/4HANA.
  4. SAP Activate Methodology: A structured approach (Prepare, Explore, Realize, Deploy) for project execution.
  5. Custom Code Migration App: Helps adapt ABAP code for S/4HANA and cloud.
  6. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): A cloud platform for extensions, integrations, and innovations (e.g., building apps with ABAP RAP, hosting AI models). Supports the “clean core” principle—keeping S/4HANA standard while offloading customizations to BTP.
  7. Embedded Analytics and Intelligence: Tools like SAP Analytics Cloud and embedded AI capabilities (e.g., predictive analytics in S/4HANA) for real-time insights.
  8. Managed Services: SAP handles upgrades, patches, and maintenance, shifting responsibility from the customer to SAP and its ecosystem partners.

How RISE with SAP Works in Practice

Imagine a company running ECC on-premise with custom ABAP code, complex integrations, and aging hardware. RISE with SAP offers a streamlined path:

  • Assessment: Use Readiness Check to analyze ECC—say, identifying 500 custom programs needing rework.
  • Strategy: Choose a migration approach (Greenfield, Brownfield, or Selective Data Transition) and a cloud model (e.g., Azure Private Cloud).
  • Execution: Leverage Migration Cockpit for data, BTP for custom apps, and SAP Activate for project management.
  • Operation: SAP manages the Azure-hosted S/4HANA instance, applying quarterly updates and ensuring 99.9% uptime.

The subscription covers licensing, infrastructure, and services, with costs based on factors like user count, system size, and cloud provider.

ABAP and Technical Perspective

For an SAP Technical Architect with ABAP expertise, RISE with SAP shifts the focus:

  1. Code Modernization: Legacy ABAP (e.g., BDCs, obsolete FMs must be rewritten for S/4HANA (e.g., CDS views, AMDP, RAP). The Custom Code Migration App flags issues, but architects design solutions—like moving a Z-report to a HANA-optimized CDS view exposed via OData.
  2. Clean Core: Customizations move to BTP, requiring ABAP developers to master RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming) or CAP (Cloud Application Programming) instead of modifying S/4HANA directly.
  3. Cloud Mindset: Programs must be stateless and scalable—e.g., replacing file-based I/O with API calls to BTP’s Integration Suite.
  4. Performance: HANA’s in-memory power demands ABAP optimizations like AMDP for heavy calculations, as seen in my earlier examples.

Challenges and Considerations

  • Vendor Lock-In: Tying into SAP’s ecosystem limits flexibility—e.g., switching hyperscalers mid-project is tough.
  • Customization Limits: Public Cloud enforces standardization, challenging companies with heavy ECC customizations.
  • Learning Curve: Teams must adapt to BTP, RAP, and cloud operations.
  • Cost: While predictable, the subscription can be pricier for smaller firms versus on-premise ECC.

RISE with SAP is SAP’s answer to the cloud era, pushing customers toward S/4HANA ahead of ECC’s 2027 support end. It’s a game-changer for architects, blending technical mastery (ABAP, HANA) with strategic planning (cloud, process redesign).

entry.title: CDS: How to use AnalyticsDetails.query.ignoreFurtherFilter in Analytical query (Constant Selection)
entry.content:
This blog is about how to use @ AnalyticsDetails.query.ignoreFurtherFilter in Analytical query to define a constant selection as like in BW query (see Note  944815).

‘Ignore filter’ is a property that influences the effect of filters on cells, in classic BW it is called as ‘Constant selection’. It is used to define a selection in query definition as fixed value. Navigation and filters then have no effect on this selection at runtime.

This feature is useful to calculate e.g., Market Index.  More information about constant selection can be found Constant Selection | SAP Help Portal

 

@ AnalyticsDetails.query.ignoreFurtherFilter

annotation is used to model 'constant selection' in the Analytical Engine.

The value of each cell in a query result set is determined by different filters acting on this cell. These filters are from the structure element or key figures itself or global filters and drill down.  

Sometimes there is a need that not all filters should be applied, therefore with this annotation filters on certain dimensions or on all dimensions can be excluded.

@AnalyticsDetails.query.ignoreFurtherFilter.forElement: Global filters and filters from drill down will not be applied on the structure element for the elements listed in the array.

@AnalyticsDetails.query.ignoreFurtherFilter.forAllElements: No global filters and no filter from drill down will be applied on the structure element.

 

Example

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_ALLOWED
@EndUserText.label: 'IGNOREFILTER- constant selection'
@ObjectModel.modelingPattern: #ANALYTICAL_QUERY
@ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities: [#ANALYTICAL_QUERY]
define transient view entity ZLR_PC_IGNOREFILTER1
provider contract analytical_query
as projection on ZDMO_I_FLIGHTCUBE
{
  
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS 
  AirlineID,

  @AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS 
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.totals: #SHOW
  ConnectionID,

  
  CurrencyCode,
   
  
  @EndUserText.label: 'Price in 2024  '
  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
   case when FlightYear = '2024' then Price else null end as priceIn2024_without,
   
   
  @EndUserText.label: 'Price in 2024 for Airline'
  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.ignoreFurtherFilter.forElement: ['ConnectionID' ]
  case when FlightYear = '2024' then Price else null end as priceIn2024
 
}
where AirlineID = 'AA' or AirlineID = 'AZ'

 

Output

In the above example column 'Price in 2024 for Airline' shows the total price for airline, as 'ConnectionID' filter is ignored with annotation @AnalyticsDetails.query.ignoreFurtherFilter.forElement: ['ConnectionID' ]

Constraint

This feature is only available in Analytical query

entry.title: AWS Mail configuration for SAP ABAP Systems
entry.content:
Introduction:-

If you have moved your SAP system to AWS, you may want to eliminate some mail servers that are still operating on-premises or avoid the deployment of an EC2 instance dedicated to running an SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol) server. Sending outgoing emails is one of the most common requirements when running a SAP system. These emails can come from a variety of sources, including workflows, batch processing and process chains, monitoring and alerting etc.

Amazon Simple Email Service (SES)

Customers can send emails from within any application with Amazon Simple Email Service (SES), which is affordable, flexible, and scalable. You can quickly configure Amazon SES to support a variety of email use cases, such as transactional, marketing, or mass email communications. In this blog, we'll walk you through the necessary steps to set up outbound mail from a SAP ABAP system using Amazon SES (Simple Email Service) service. 

Prerequisites:-

In order to do preliminary testing, we will first set up a "Sandbox" account in Amazon SES and confirm a sender email address.  All emails originating from our SAP systems will be accepted by the SES service once all setup procedures have been completed successfully and this account has been converted to production.

Go to Amazon SES in the AWS EC2 Console, choose Email Addresses, and then click "Verify a New Email Address."  Please check your mail inbox after entering your email address and clicking Verify This Email Address.

After the validation is completed, the Status will change to green

Once the email address verification is completed, we need to create proper SMTP credentials which will be used by our SAP systems. To create the credentials, click on “SMTP Settings” and press the “Create My SMTP Credentials” button.

Note the Server Name as it will be required afterwards during the SAP system configuration

Enter a username and click on create bottom in the bottom

Download the SMTP username and password credentials in a csv file

SAP ABAP Outbound Email Configuration:-

Go to ABAP system and call transaction SCOT, select the SMTP nodes, and create a new one via the wizard

Click on the settings button, and provide the credentials generated in the previous steps

Download the certificate from the Amazon SES SMTP endpoint (for example using this cmd line on a Linux system : openssl s_client -starttls smtp -connect email-smtp.us-east-1.amazonaws.com:587 | sed -ne ‘/-BEGIN CERTIFICATE-/,/-END CERTIFICATE-/p’)

Note: the Amazon SES endpoints are region dependent, so please ensure you are using the one within the same region.

In transaction STRUST, import the certificate into the SSL client (Standard) and restart the service via SMICM

Check outbound email:-

In transaction SO01, create a test email and press the send button

In transaction SOST, We can trigger a send job via menu Send Request à Start Send Process for Selection.

Conclusion:-

In this blog, we have demonstrated how to set up SAP ABAP systems to send out emails, which is a frequent requirement from the standpoints of business processes and base operations.

Related information:-

https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/awsforsap/category/sap/ 

https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/awsforsap/simplify-sap-jobs-scheduling-using-aws-native-tooling/ 

Please share your feedback and your thoughts. Write back to me for questions and queries.

Thank you.
